input.initialPrompt: Analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for this project. I haven't run any coverage yet.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for this project. They haven't run any coverage yet, so I need to first understand the project structure, then set up and run tests with coverage, and finally analyze the results to identify risk hotspots.

Let me start by exploring the project structure to understand what we're working with.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with code coverage collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with code coverage collection

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture of the project. It's a .NET 9 solution with:
- A main project (`ContosoUniversity`) containing `StudentService.cs` with 4 methods: `Enroll`, `CalculateGpa`, `FindById`, `Search`
- A test project (`ContosoUniversity.UnitTests`) with MSTest and coverlet.collector

The test project already has `coverlet.collector` which can collect code coverage. Let me run the tests with coverage collection and then analyze the results.

I'll run `dotnet test` with coverage collection enabled.

arguments.description: Run tests with code coverage collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-a56a91740b6647e998df89061070644f && dotnet test --collect:\"XPlat Code Coverage\" --results-directory ./TestResults 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with code coverage collection", "initial_wait": 60}
